predictions.description: After set alpha, set beta, overwrite alpha, set token key gamma: keys are alpha;beta;gamma — insertion order preserved, overwriting an existing key does NOT create a duplicate, and both key and value token substitution worked (gamma came from {var:seedKey}).
Reading alpha back via the key: pipe yields the second write's value, not the first.
hasKey:beta reports True (engine boolean formatting) while beta is present.
remove-from-dictionary drops exactly beta: remaining keys alpha;gamma keep relative order and hasKey:beta flips to False.
Removing a key that is not in the dictionary passes as a no-op (ignoreErrors NOT set on that step) and leaves the keys unchanged.
clear-dictionary leaves zero keys; the snapshot variable started as SENTINEL so the 0 proves the pipe actually ran against the cleared dictionary.
